pattern: Each cell's count of strictly increasing paths ending there equals one plus the sum of the counts of its strictly-smaller neighbours, computed with memoized search over the grid.
hints: Count paths that end at each cell; a single-cell path always exists, contributing the plus one.
A path ending at a cell can extend from any orthogonal neighbour with a strictly smaller value, so add up those neighbours' counts.
Memoize per cell because the dependency graph is acyclic by strictly-increasing value; sum every cell's count under the modulus.

edge_inputs.description: single cell has exactly one path
equal neighbours do not extend a strictly increasing path
plateau of equal values yields only length-one paths

stress.description: random small grid with values drawn from a modest range to create ties and chains
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    cap = max(1, min(size, 12))
    m = rng.randint(1, cap)
    n = rng.randint(1, cap)
    hi = rng.randint(2, 9)
    grid = [[rng.randint(1, hi) for _ in range(n)] for _ in range(m)]
    return [grid]
stress.note: small dimensions keep the exponential oracle fast
